benefits: Coccyganthe flos-cuculi, also known as the marsh marigold or kingcup, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. intro: Coccyganthe flos-cuculi, also known as marsh marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is rich in health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coccyganthe flos-cuculi include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Marsh marigold is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
